skill.name: security-requirement-extraction
skill.description: Derive testable security requirements from threat models, business context, and compliance needs.
skill.summary: Turn threats and compliance goals into security requirements, user stories, tests, and traceability matrices.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-severity patterns, but manual review found they are false positives from Markdown formatting, defensive security terminology, and reference links. No prompt injection, credential access, executable command path, network exfiltration, or malicious intent was found. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from community-authored sample code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged backticks are Markdown code fences or inline resource paths. No shell command execution, subprocess call, or user-controlled command path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain Markdown formatting and Python documentation examples, not Ruby backtick execution. No executable skill script or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Security Vocabulary Misclassified as Malicious Behavior
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Terms related to cryptography, SOC2, network security, tokens, and testing appear in requirement templates. They describe defensive requirements and do not access credentials or perform reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is defensive documentation for extracting requirements from threats. The flagged words are labels, compliance names, and sample requirement text.

low_findings.title: Reference Links Misclassified as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The URLs are static citations to OWASP, NIST, and an OReilly book. The skill does not fetch these URLs or send data to an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is limited to Markdown hyperlinks in a resources section. No HTTP client, fetch call, webhook, or encoded outbound payload was found.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Dismissed After Review
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The critical heuristic combined unrelated false positives: Markdown code fences, security-domain examples, and reference links. No combined code execution, network transfer, or credential access flow exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no semantic link between command execution, network access, and credential access. The files are static Markdown guidance and defensive sample code.

content.value_statement: Threat models often stop at risks without clear delivery tasks. This skill turns threats, business goals, and compliance inputs into testable security requirements.
actual_capabilities: Converts STRIDE-style threat inputs into security requirements.
Creates security user stories with acceptance criteria.
Maps requirements to compliance frameworks such as PCI DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, and OWASP.
Generates security test case ideas from each requirement.
Builds traceability between threats, requirements, and controls.
Prioritizes requirements by impact and likelihood.
limitations: Does not validate legal compliance for a regulated organization.
Requires accurate threat and business context from the user.
Provides templates and guidance, not a running requirements platform.
Does not replace architecture review or penetration testing.

use_cases.title: Convert Threat Models Into Backlog Items
use_cases.description: Turn identified threats into security user stories, acceptance criteria, and test ideas for sprint planning.

use_cases.title: Prepare Compliance Traceability
use_cases.description: Map security requirements to common compliance controls and identify coverage gaps for audit preparation.

use_cases.title: Define Secure Design Requirements
use_cases.description: Create clear requirements for authentication, authorization, logging, validation, and data protection before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Requirements
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner extraction from a short threat summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to convert the following threat summary into security requirements. Include priority, rationale, acceptance criteria, and test ideas.

prompt_templates.title: Build Security User Stories
prompt_templates.scenario: Product team planning work from known risks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to turn these threats into user stories for an engineering backlog. Include definition of done and traceability to each threat.

prompt_templates.title: Map Requirements to Compliance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to map these security requirements to PCI DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, OWASP, or NIST controls. Highlight missing or weak coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Full Requirements Package
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a new system or major feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a complete security requirements package from this business context, architecture notes, threat model, and compliance scope.

output_examples.input: A payment API threat model lists spoofing, tampering, and information disclosure risks.
output_examples.output: The skill produces prioritized authentication, integrity, and encryption requirements with acceptance criteria and test cases.

output_examples.input: A team needs GDPR and OWASP coverage for a customer profile service.
output_examples.output: The skill returns requirements grouped by data protection, authorization, logging, and input validation, with compliance traceability notes.

output_examples.input: A product manager has vague security goals for a new admin console.
output_examples.output: The skill turns the goals into clear user stories for access control, session handling, audit logging, and secure error handling.

best_practices: Provide threats, affected assets, business impact, and compliance scope before extraction.
Ask for acceptance criteria and test cases for every high-priority requirement.
Review generated requirements with security, engineering, product, and compliance stakeholders.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic requirements without threat or system context.
Do not treat generated compliance mappings as legal or audit approval.
Do not skip prioritization when requirements affect delivery planning.

faq.question: What inputs work best with this skill?
faq.answer: Threat models, business goals, architecture notes, data classifications, compliance scope, and known security constraints work best.

faq.question: Can it create security user stories?
faq.answer: Yes. It can format requirements as user stories with acceptance criteria, test cases, priority, and traceability.

faq.question: Does it support compliance mapping?
faq.answer: Yes. The playbook includes mappings for common frameworks, but final mapping should be reviewed by compliance experts.

faq.question: Can it replace a threat model?
faq.answer: No. It works best after threats are identified, then converts those threats into actionable requirements.

faq.question: Does it run code or connect to services?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill is Markdown guidance with examples and does not execute commands or make network requests.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
